user_comment: This suit fit me so nicely! I HATE shopping for suits because I am smaller chested and nothing ever fits right or comfortably. But this fits like a glove and is so comfortable! The bottom back is more cheeky than I prefer but nothing some swim shorts can't cover. For reference I am 5'4", 120lbs, and 36 AA..... UPDATED REVIEW After wearing this 1 time for a few hours in the lake, the unadjustable straps stretched out really badly making the top no longer snug to my chest. The bottom piece also stretched out really badly making it so I have to wear swim shorts with it now... after washing it shrunk back to normal but stretched right back out in the same places after an hour or 2 of wear..... for the price I DEFINITELY expected better quality but that's on me I guess...

When I first saw and liked this bathing suit, I was hesitant due to the typical age-appropriate stereotypes associated with swimwear. At 68, I was tired of the limited options marketed for "older women", and no, I didn't want to wear one of those, often recommended, bathing suits with a skirt down to my knees. This bathing suit proved to be a game-changer. It defies stereotypes, allowing us seniors to embrace body-positive fashion for women of all ages.This bathing suit caught my eye, even though it was showcased on a young, well-toned model. Despite not resembling the young model, this suit made me feel comfortable and empowered. For reference, I'm 5'4, 115-120lbs, and the size small fits me perfectly, so be sure to order your regular size. The fear of wedged bottoms or camel-toe is non-existent with this suit. The ruched pattern on the torso conceals any stomach imperfections, and the suit offers excellent support. The quality is outstanding, with strong stitching and well-designed structure. Made with mesh lining, nylon and spandex, it elegantly molds to curves and provides excellent coverage. The push-up bra with removable paddding adds versatility, allowing for customization.The long zipper at the back, aided by a convenient attached string makes it easy to put on solo. I love its versatility. It easily transitions from beachwear to an elegant top, pairing seamlessly with jeans, pants, or a skirt. This bathing suit is a celebration of beauty at any age, size, or shape. Very reasonably priced, it's a must-have for those seeking comfort, style, and positive, age-defying statement. Highly recommended 👌

(NOT A PHOTO OF ME JUST A FEW IDEAS OTHER THAN WHAT IS POSTED!!!) This swimsuit is stunning. I absolutely love the way it fits me. I recently lost 35 pounds and I’m totally feeling myself in this! I am 5’5”, 185lbs, and ordered a size XL. This is going to be my “go to” swimsuit for my family vacation, bachelorette party I’m attending in the fall, and just hanging by the pool. I cannot express how much confidence this swimsuit gave me. I’m in love!
Bought just for my cruise in Oct. I absolutely loved it and felt confident wearing it. XL 195 5'7 Recommend!
Bought this swimsuit for a Vegas trip it fits perfect. For reference I am 5ft 5 192lbs and I wear an XL so it's true to size. Also have 4 kids so it hides my mummy tummy well
Bought this swimsuit for a Vegas trip it fits perfect. For reference I am 5ft 5 192lbs and I wear an XL so it's true to size. Also have 4 kids so it hides my mummy tummy well

I absolutely loved this suit. I ordered an XL im about 230lbs. I got plenty of compliments from this suit. It definitely exposes a lot of booty. But I have a large butt so there’s that. I definitely recommend.

I got this to wear beneath my rashguard as I'm very busty and I NEED support. Fabric: This fabric is very soft to the touch and feels great on the skin. It isn't itchy or irritating and honestly feels softer than some other suits I own. Quality: The top is fully lined, which is a must for swimwear. The lining fabric appears to be the same as the outer material. There is also a mesh lining in the front which I can only assume is for breathability. It seems to be very well-made with no flaws in stitching. No loose threads or uneven seams. Fit: My underbust measurement is 33", overbust is 41.5", and I wear a US 34H bra. I ordered the XL and the band offers quite a snug and supportive fit. While it is snug, it isn't so much so to the point of being uncomfortable. I prefer a snug fit in the band so I don't have to worry and underboob and slipping out the bottom. I do wish the straps were adjustable and that there was a little bit more coverage in front. But it's fine since I'll be wearing this beneath something and it won't be seen anyway. I'll have to see how well it wears when wet and for a prolonged period of time. But for now, I think this was a good option.

I love this cover! I am 5'8" and 200lbs and the XL fits perfectly. It is light, flowy and breathable on the skin but it also shields my skin. It doesn't make me feel like I am wearing a trench coat, which is exactly what I was going for. 2 of my 3 bathing suits have black in them so it matches great. It is sewn together well, even with it getting wet at the splash pad with the kiddos and being washed 3 or 4 times a week, it has held up great for about a month now. Very happy with this purchase!

I love this coverup! I am 5’6”, size 14, 190 lbs & wearing an XL. I’ve taken it to Mexico twice now & worn it in the hot OK summer and it doesn’t get too hot or anything for being long sleeve. Breathes well and looks great. Looks like Mumu for a lot less. Do NOT dry this after washing. Let it air dry. Made that mistake & had to repurchase. Worth it though!

I normally wear a large in tops and bathing suits and have a fairly large bust (38D), but based on other reviews I sized up and got an extra large. The top itself appears to be well made, has good stitching and the shoulders are adjustable. The back straps are also cute and sexy. The main issue for me is the built-in bust support. It’s just not quite large enough to support my girls. If you don’t mind showing cleavage, this is great. If you want more coverage, it may not be for you.

I was super surprised at how this fits. I am 5’ 4” tall and a C cup bra. I weigh 185 lbs. I ordered size XL thinking the top might not fit but the bottom would. Turns out they both fit great. I love it & get lots of compliments.

I was surprised that I could actually order a swimsuit online, but after reading the reviews and viewing photos I took the plunge! 💦 I’m 5’3’ 165 and over 50! The only thing I would like more of is a snugger fit in the backside/leg holes of the swim bottoms.

I’m a 38DD, 5’9”, and about 180. I’ve had two kids…need I say more. I’m including pictures (with the cellulite, because let’s keep this real) so y’all can see it is a lovely suit. I had a black suit that I loved for a long time but the elastic finally bit the dust, enter this lovely lady. Ruching in the right places, no halter that kills my neck, just a touch of vintage. I’m giving her 5 stars. Stop reading the next 70 reviews and just go buy it.

It helped me feel comfortably covered up but still cute and not frumpy. I’m 5’1 so the back went to my knees and the front, just above. The arms weren’t tight. It is see through as it shows but not too much. I really like it.

Ordered a size up from my normal size and the fit is great. I don't like my swim wear tight. I am 180 lbs 5'7" and a 38B. I ordered an XL. This top is well made and comfortable. The straps are adjustable and the style is flattering and slimmimg. Very happy with this swim top.

Stylish bathing suit at very affordable price. I took off one star only because it isn’t a perfect fit, as I’ve gained weight due to a medical condition, and the bust area I have to adjust a bit to make it look right. I like the ruching in the middle to hide imperfections :). I am 5’10, about 195 lbs, 38DD. This suit is NOT underwire, just FYI. I think it would look better on me if I hadn’t put on so much tummy/upper thigh weight, and perhaps had a bit of a smaller bust. But, that being said, I like it, it fit true to size (I wear between 14-16 dep on brand but always go up in suits so I got size 16 here), and the black is slimming.

I am 5'3, 112lbs and an A cup. I ordered the XS and it fit perfectly. I sometimes struggle finding suits on Amazon as even the smaller sizes seem to always have a D cup or higher. This one isn't like that so rejoice petite/ smaller chested ladies! With at said, there is still ample stretch for those with larger chest. I would estimate the XS would accommodate up to a small C without being too risque. The removable pads are a little sad though. It's not full cup. It's those little circle pads that almost always look awkward. I will be replacing those with something better. The shape is very flattering and it's adjustable on the shoulders which I like. Overall it's a great top and I would definitely recommend to my smaller ladies.

This suit is gorgeous beyond words. I’m usually a bikini gal and if I have to do a one piece I get LaBlanca, but this suit is so beautiful and on par with the quality of $100+ suit. I wore it to a dressy pool event on a cruise. I got a ton of compliments. I am 5’ 8”, 140 pounds and wore a SMALL . not that it matters but I’m 52 years old and to be honest I’m starting to feel a little more comfortable in a one piece. I plan to purchase this in white for next summer. I really wish they made it in a true red. Get this suit you will not regret it.
Fits me perfect. I’m 180 pounds, 38 ddd. Ordered a 2XL. The size chart is very accurate since I am a size 12/14. I just had a 4 month old baby and this hides the mommy pouch well.

I am so happy with this purchase! We all know the dreaded swimsuit purchase on Amazon usually does NOT go well. Between trying to get the right size and then waiting weeks for the arrival from China just to find out the suit isn't what was advertised or fits like it was made for a 4'11" size 00 person just makes me want to give up on online suit shopping! This year I had my heart set on a lemon suit and after trying on a suit at TJ Maxx that was beyond hysterical (it was cut so high it showed my whole lemon 😂) and a suit at Dillard's that was approximately 9 million dollars, I had just about given up. Then I saw this suit on Amazon. I thought, "Nah, it won't fit, and by the time it gets here summer will be over," but after a week of thinking I decided to give it a try since it was only $25. I normally wear a size 12ish so I ordered the XXL (size 14) thinking it would probably be best to buy slightly bigger. I hit order and to my delight it said it would be delivered in 2 DAYS! What!?! No 3 week wait? Oh my sweet heavens to Betsy was I pumped about that! Well the suit arrived today and it fits like a dream! It covers my whole bootie and has a lovely high waist. The top looks amazing as off the shoulder or strappless and if you take your arms out of the sleeves it looks like a whole different suit! I could not be happier! I might even go buy another print I love it so much!

Ok so despite having a mom bod that was totally trashed by having Irish twins, I still want to make memories and not feel so uncomfortable (in a one piece) I loved my tankini last year but am trying to promote body positivity and acceptance. I feel like this is a great compromise. It's still modest enough to not feel inappropriate at the pool with the kids but the slinky off shoulder top makes me feel like I could cuddle with hubby at the beach. It fit perfectly . I'm 180 and 5'8 36dd. I got the xxl. The only reason I didn't give 5 starts is because I felt the leg holes could be a little better. It just kind of pushed on my legs weird but not enough for me to not wear it or loved how I feel in it. I might not look or feel like a supermodel but I feel confident I can play with my little boys in the sand and set a good example for my teenage daughter.

I’ve tried the Amazon knockoff that’s cheaper and it’s just not the same as the Becca quality. This wtap coverup/dress is simple and vibrant. It's easy to store in a bag for the beach or pool. And covers enough to be able to go to a restaurant or run errands after the beach comfortably. The butterfly is very vibrant and stands out making it feel like summer wear. The product is made well. And the adjustable straps allow for it to cover any bathing suit style. Great suit for older women looking for a more modest one piece suit! The fabric has some stretch and ample coverage plus the back is higher and more flattering than most. I am 5’3 and have a pear shaped figure and a short torso. This is a size 6-8 and I normally wear an 8.

Great suit for older women looking for a more modest one piece suit! The fabric has some stretch and ample coverage plus the back is higher and more flattering than most. I am 5’3 and have a pear shaped figure and a short torso. This is a size 6-8 and I normally wear an 8.

Loved the fit and comfort of the shorts! I wasn't worried about anything riding up or hanging out. Very comfy for beach activities. The colors were pretty and matched the listing for the blue/white BLUE FEATHER suit ***Was NOT the case for the aqua GREEN PINEAPPLE. That one appeared aqua/mint on my monitor but was actually an earthy sage. I ordered 3 of these bathing suits. Blue feather, green pineapple, and white leaves. Prints don't line up perfectly on blue feather shorts but it wasn't bad enough I would've returned over it. My dislike was the fit of the top for my breasts. I'm a natural 34B post kids. Some support is beneficial for me and this top offered none. Although it's adjustable the chest band part isn't elastic so it just kinda sits there vs cinching in. Also I don't have much cleavage and, as you can see from top view, it left some gaping at the center for me. I tried both size S and M and the tops fit nearly identically. The only difference I could tell was tightness in the waist of the bottoms. Maybe my boobs aren't big enough for this top. IDK. I ended up keeping the white leaves suit to pair its shorts with a different top I already owned.

This swimsuit is flattering on the "mom" figure! It hides the imperfections perfectly! The print is cute without looking childish. The bust line gives enough coverage for a larger bust without looking dowdy. The built in skirt gives the coverage for the bottom that we tend to need "at a certain age". While the cute tie at the hip keeps it youthful and feminine! I LOVE this swimsuit! For reference, I'm 5'3" 160#, bust 40", waist 35", hips 40". And I ordered a size Large.

My bra size is a 38 C and I ordered the 16 plus and it fits perfectly, I’m able to put it on over my head without taking the buckle apart on the back. The buckle clicks in place and seems to come apart easily, but there is a solid strap below this so the suit would not come apart, it also seems to hold well when on. The cups are padded and there is no spillage anywhere when on. This bikini top works so well with my swim shorts.

Ive been hiding my body for years but I'm moving to a building with a pool and i decided it was time to buy a swimsuit. Admittedly i wear an 18 in jeans and a 44 dd cacique bra. I ordered this suit in an 18w and a 20w. The 20W is perfect. Definitely buying a second one right now.
Perfect fit. I thought the bottom would be too big, but they were just great. Adjustable and very flattering. I'm 5'3 225 and ordered a size 20W.

I am 5’7” and weigh 270lbs. I normally wear a size 20 jeans and my bra size is a 42G. From the previous reviews I ordered one size up and got a 22. I’ve had several children so my stomach pretty big and I have big thighs. This swimsuit surprised me. It was way more flattering than I expected. The top does not have support so I plan to just wear a swim bra (not pictured). The design of the top hides my love handles and stomach which I love. I’ll post an update when i actually wear it to the beach next week and will be able to comment more on durability.

Jim's wife here. As you can see I'm thiccck! So finding a good suit that is pretty, fits well and covers my all "assets" is not an easy feat. I'm always apprehensive when ordering online since you can't try it on before purchasing, but I always look at the sizing chart and go based on the measurements. I've got it all, big boobs, big butt, a big belly, wide hips, and thick legs. Speaking of, please excuse the weird tan on my legs. We went fishing and I was wearing capris. 😅 So, based on the measurements, I'm somewhere between a 2XL and a 3XL. I always size up because I'd rather it be a bit big vs being too tight. I have a 45" bust and 54" hips, and 45" waist, which doesn't really matter for this type of suit. Based on this, I ordered the 3XL. The suit was scheduled to arrive on a Tuesday, which unfortunately was already a day too late to make it on time for our vacation, my fault though as I waited too long to place the order. However, what wasn't my fault was that the suit was shipped out a whole day later than the scheduled time so it ended up getting delivered on Wednesday. So that was disappointing. I recommend you order a week in advance if you want it in on time for a trip. About the suit. I haven't worn it to the pool yet but based on how it fits and feels on me now I don't foresee any issues. It feels amazing. It is so soft and comfortable. The cup inserts are nice and thick and cover the breast fully and give a nice gentle lift. Usually, I end up taking them out because they're too small but not this time around. These are fantastic! The bodice is great and my hubby pointed out that it doesn't make my sides bulge out which is awesome. The suit and booty shorts fit as expected. The shorts are double layered so you won't have issues with them being see through and they cover my butt fully. They fit and feel great. Although, I bought a second pair of swim bottoms because I wanted something with longer legs to prevent my thighs from chafing. I am noticing though the the inside part of the legs on the booty shorts are rolling up as I sit here on my couch, so I'm glad that I got the longer "biker style" swim shorts. All in all I think this is a great buy. I am not disappointed at all with the feel or quality of the suit. Plus I absolutely love that it looks like a little dress that I can comfortably walk around in and not feel self conscious about it being a bathing suit. I do wish they offered a longer short, but that's okay. If I had skinnier legs booty shorts would be just fine. Rating: I'm giving 4 stars. If the suit had arrived on time, as projected, I would give it 5 stars but since it was a day late, I deducted a star. All in all I recommend this suit/seller.

I will say if you have a long torso or large breasts, I don’t recommend. I didn’t feel much cup support. Overall I really like it going to keep it. I usually wear size 18-20 in clothes and dresses. I ordered a size 22 in this suit.

I am 5’9”, 160lbs, wear a size 10 pants (30/32) and have a 38C cup. I ordered a large of this tummy controlled one piece bathing suit and where as it mostly fits, this product has a lot of issues. As shown in the photos, many of the stitches are uneven or different lengths which causes odd lumps, rolls or places this suit sits oddly. It gaps at the breast side even with the shoulder straps tightened till then dig into the skin. The waist strap has several areas that the stitching is not straight which causes the fabric to roll on itself, making folds that do not flatter the form. The back does not sit flat because the stitching on the elastic is to tight, pulling at the fabric. Where as this bathing suit does have a mesh liner on the front bottom part, it offers no true tummy support and does not offer any compression. Instead, because it is only on the front half and not all the way around, it causes the fabric around the rump and lower back to be to thin, causing wrinkles and bunching. The pads in the breast are round and do not offer any real support or stability and for a middle aged woman both are needed with softer breast tissue. This causes the need for adjustment often. The straps placement makes it difficult to shorten or lengthen the straps without having to partially undress or risk breaking seams. The back tie is for looks only, not really assisting in holding the bathing suit to the body but needs to be tied to feel larger breasts from traveling. This bathing suit had beautiful potential yet fell far from the mark with the lack of attention to detail. This suit may work better on smaller chested, younger people but it is not something I would be comfortable wearing.

I am so excited for this suit for the summer! I ordered my usual size and it fits (but, is a bit compressing). The straps tripped me up putting them on (got super tangled, LOL). Hides all the unflattering lumps and makes me look snatched (is that still a word? lol) Highly recommend. I'm 5'5", 185lbs, 36C and got a Large.

Ok. I'm not one to post bikini pics publicly, BUT...when I say I'm impressed with CUPSHE bikinis, I...AM...IMPRESSED! This is the first time I've bought a bikini that's not a mix-n-match when it comes to sizing and I bought 2 from CUPSHE based on other reviews with photos/measurements, etc, and I love both! So, I hope I'm able to pay it forward... I'm 5'5", 117lb, more junk in the trunk than treasure in the chest, so I normally have to buy a size larger in bottoms. I went with a medium and it was perfect! (In both that I purchased!) I feel body-confident in it, it doesn't ride up as I move, it appears well-made (no poor stitching), no snags in the ribbed outer material, and the color is beautiful. Another small bonus is how the tags are attached. I'm a tag remover and these were perfect ~ attached by a string, rather than the tag directly being attached to the bikini itself. Easily removed with no itchy scratchy factor from leftover tag bits. It arrived with all tags attached, hygienic liner in place, and in a zipper type plastic bag. I definitely recommend CUPSHE and will probably be purchasing more myself soon!

5’6 - 180-190lbs - size 10/12 - curvy - 38DD, 34” waste line, 41” hips - L/XL Ordered an XL This is a looking cute sitting on the beach or by the pool bikini, probably wouldn’t swim in this. This bikini has great stretch with providing moderate support overall. The top is just a little small on me but the girls stay in place! I can lift my arms up, move them around, walk, bend over, and they don’t spill out but you can see my under boob sticking out the bottom but I don’t really mind it because they stay in place. I will probably be adjusting myself every so often and checking to make sure my chest isn’t entirely exposed. The top is one piece of fabric and doesn’t have any clasps or adjustment straps. The set feels more like a bralette and panty which gives it that comfortability. The bottoms have a nice thick band and doesn't cut into anything and it goes high enough to sit over my belly. The bottoms are very cheeky but I feel covered in the right places. The bottoms also fit slightly bigger in the back so the fabric bunches a little. I might order a size L just to see if the bottom fits better but I definitely wouldn’t fit in a L top. I am going to rerun this because of the color and see how a L/XL black set works when I mix the sizes. it’s a great price point so I don’t doing that.

The attached photos show reality of the fit and look, unlike the photo-shopped ad. The size Medium fits okay when dry (5' 4", 120#); I wouldn't want it any tighter, but don't want it too loose before getting wet. The straps and back are a comfortable design so you don't worry about the suit falling or shifting with activity. The bottom rides up just a little before getting wet, the top trim and piping does not sit flat; could be a sewing issue or may vary with body shape.

Not feeling this one. My measurements were not ideal for this. The pants fit, the band size was way too big, the bust was just the right size but was sewn together poorly, and the shirt was too big. In other words if you're big endowed, this will not fit. The sewing was really messy and contributed to a sloppy look. The elastic was not attached securely in the chest band and had flipped around at some point during production. The seams in the front also randomly decided to flip sides. Since the bust didn't have any form or support to it, the top looked very blobby all around and made my breasts sit wide in a very unflattering way. The band was also so loose that in the process of taking the shirt on and off, I slipped out from underneath. This bra top is not suitable for anyone with a big difference in band and cup size (I'm a 38 H for reference) On the tank top there were these weird little stitches on either side of the breast which did nothing but make it look crumpled up. The pants fit fine, but don't have any kind of liner and since they're a bit loose at the leg I'd want to wear underwear or a bikini bottom under them (not included) The top was also see through. There is an extra layer to prevent seeing nip but you can clearly see the extra layer, the pads, and the tag. The fabric is very see through and water will only make this worse. Ultimately I feel like this would perform very poorly in the pool and I would look like a mess wearing it. The black ones might hide these design flaws better but overall this is a cheap poorly made product. I'll be returning if possible.

I loved this swimsuit from the pictures online because it looked long enough to cover everything and still be super cute. I was not disappointed! I am apple shaped with a bigger stomach and a smallish chest and usually swimsuits are either too short, the chest area is too big, the shorts that come with it don’t fit, or they just aren’t flattering. Well, this swimsuit was none of that! It fits amazingly like a short dress which sold me right out the gate. The chest area fits great, especially since the straps are adjustable. I may end up taking the padding out just because it doesn’t stay in place very well. The top is not too tight around my bust before opening up and flowing down and being very flattering to whatever shape your tummy is. I wasn’t even going to bother trying on the shorts it comes with, but I’m glad I did! They’re actually a good length so I can wear them over my stomach like usual and still have some material left for my legs. Personally I don’t mind short shorts or showing off my legs/skin so both pieces of this swimsuit are a win to me. I may end up buying another one in a different pattern! For sizing help, I purchased the 18 plus and my normal sizes are: 38-40C/L-XL bra; L-XL mens tops, XL-2XL womens tops; 16-18/XL-XXL pants/dresses

For perspective, I’m 5’7”, 125 lbs, and really flat chested with a decent shaped backside. I got the medium and it fit perfectly. Even though I’m really flat chested, I think the small would’ve actually been too small for me. Also, I was able to fit my gel inserts into the bra part (it does come with a small bra insert but that’s not enough for me). I loved this bathing suit when I first saw it and I love it still. Great quality, nice fabric, so cute, love the reverse bottom option, and the ties on the sides of the bikini bottoms and on the back of the bikini top are really cute. So flattering from the front but… did not personally work for my backside. No one, absolutely no one, needs to see that much of my @$$. It was riding up on me while I was just standing there taking these pics. I can’t imagine where it would’ve ended up after walking around the pool. I like my butt, and I don’t even care about the cellulite, but I’m personally not interested in putting all of my goods out there on display. You can see from my tan lines that my bikini bottoms typically cover a bit more. But that’s just my personal taste. I know the new styles are more cheeky… I just can’t do it. If you have a great tight, firm, and perky butt, you need to rock this suit because it’s adorable. If you like the cheeky styles, this suit is too cute. This girl, however, wants a little more butt coverage.

For reference: I'm a 34D, 5' 3" and 137 lbs, squishy around the middle and pear shaped. Usually a size 10 in pants. M/L in tops and dresses. I went with a size medium. I wanted so badly to love this suit. The overall fit was great. The suit is so cute but there were a few things that I just couldn't get past. The suit is great quality for the money, I would have liked the top to be a bit more supportive but it still would have done the trick if the rest of the suit was perfect. The suit was overall pretty flattering and did a great job hiding/smoothing my torso lumps and bumps. Also, the green is gorgeous in person and the tie back is so adorable. The ugly: The leg holes are SO tight that I couldn't wait to get the suit off...I had red leg indents after 3 minutes of wear. Why do suit designers insist on making leg holes so tight? Also, the rear coverage just wasn't enough, it would have been wedgie city for me....to be fair, I do have a wide hinder so this may be perfect for small tushes.

I think I love this suit. I am a 5'6", 132 lb mom of three, and I'm just getting into one pieces at this point to have more coverage and mobility when at beach/pools with kiddos. It definitely has good tummy control, but I especially love the chest cut. I am a 32D bra size but the ladies fall quite low after 3 kids, so I don't have to worry about support and exposure. I could see this chest cut being an issue for those with more cleavage/less mom sag. I ordered a Medium and I feel like it fits perfectly. If I had any complaint it would be that it is very cheeky in the bum (photos show), and just fits my torso length (could be too short for 5'8" and up), but that is what I generally hear about Cupshe suits in general. But all in all... it's a winner in my book. We'll see how it holds up at the beach!

I absolutely love the cut of this bathing suit. The bottoms flatter my waist and hips, which I’ve always been self conscious of. It hides what I want it to hide and shows what I want it to show. However… I had to exchange this for a larger size. For reference, I am 5’5, 160 lbs and wear a bra size 36DD. I had to exchange a large for an extra large. I am normally a medium (size 8) in bottoms and large in tops, but I decided to opt for the large to accommodate for my boobs (gotta love women’s sizing). When I received the size large in the mail, the bottoms fit more like a medium, but were a bit snug on the waist. The top was a completely different story, it looked like a small and my boobs were FALLING out. The XL is better, but even so, I feel like I’m going to be adjusting it lot and accidentally showing people way more than what they wanted to see at the beach. Overall I love the cut and color of this suit, it’s super flattering and will definitely compliment my tan. However, I don’t think it’s very functional if you’re looking for a supportive top. This probably isn’t the best choice for the ladies with big tatas.

I have thought of buying a one piece just to have, after all a bikini does not work on waterslides. So I loved this swimsuit when I first put it on. The fit is perfect when its dry. It has tummy control and hides my stomach. The problem is when it gets wet the white lining inside makes it sag and hang weird against the skin then that causes the material of the front of the suit to act like a suction cup to your skin. The lining used is not a good combination with the type of material of the suit (Nylon and spandex) I hate that the lining is white and shows. As you can see in my photo, I had just got out of the water and the suit is just not laying right against my skin. So if you plan to use in a public pool, you will be adjusting alot and there is nothing worse than adjusting a swimsuit over and over again. For size reference I am 5'6" 155lbs 36DD chest I am wearing the large and when it's wet there isn't much chest support. Lots of sagging and weird suction due to the nylon material. I'm sad because I can't return it now but I hope this helps someone before buying!

I’m really happy with this tankini swimsuit! The tummy control feature is fantastic, offering a smooth and flattering fit without feeling too tight. The color is also exactly what I love—it’s bright but with a slightly toned-down shade, so it doesn’t stand out too much, which I appreciate. I think the swimsuit offers the perfect balance of style and comfort, and it’s great for swimming or lounging. Overall, it's a wonderful option for anyone looking for a flattering and functional swimsuit!

I rarely review items but I literally cried when I tried this swimsuit on, so it's definitely worth my time. I had a baby 5 months ago and between that, a toddler, and work, I have not been taking care of myself. I don't feel good in any clothes right now and am just really self conscious about my body. But I want to take my kids swimming this summer, so I need a suit. The pattern and color on this suit really caught my eye. First of all, the color of the swimsuit is accurate to what is shown. I was also surprised at the weight of the material; nice and thick and feels like good quality. When I looked in the mirror though I was so happy I cried. I feel so cute and a bit sexy in this swimsuit. I'm used to swimdresses looking matronly, but this one is not at all, while still modestly covering what it needs to. It's so comfortable that I want to wear it all day. For reference, I am 5'5" and 205lbs and carry most of my weight in my butt and thighs. I ordered an XL and while it isn't baggy, it's not tight either. It doesn't have "tummy control" or compression, but the ruching helps to conceal the mom tummy a bit. Oh, and it was important that I'd be able to breastfeed in this suit. I think that should be fairly easy given the cut of the top. I cannot recommend this swimsuit enough. When I do finally lose the baby weight, I will probably just buy another of these in a smaller size.

They are high waisted and not very "cheeky"; still I may just wear my swim shorts with this top and it'll still be super cute. As a reference I am 5'2" 115 lbs and I got the M. Cons- - The only con I can see thus far is the built in bra cups. They are not sewn in and they might float around in the suit too much for my liking. I might sew them in to keep them put.

Note: I ordered the XL. Dimensions right off the bat (again, for the XL size laid flat): Width shoulder strap to strap- 56 in (approx) Length middle back- 30 in Longest length (longest part of wing)- 43 in (approx) Shortest strap length- 9 in Longest strap length- 19 in I think for a swimsuit coverup I was expecting something cheaper but this is actually really nice! The colors are just as vibrant as the actual pictures, and the material is soft and stretchy. It is about as see through as solid cover wraps usually are, but not too bad because there's a inner layer that's just white, so that gives you an extra layer. I was impressed by the size of the arm holes, they fit with lots of room to spare! They can also adjust to quite big or quite small. This fit me about how I expected it to, but nowhere near according to their measurement chart. I'm about 5 ft 8 in and so the front felt a little short maybe? If you're going by the measurement chart they give, size up. Or, just buy whatever size you normally buy and ignore their chart. This is so soft I think I'll probably drape it over the back of a couch or something when not in use, it is nice to look at and touch 👍

I don't like to bend over and feel like I'm showing off all my goods. That's I felt when I tried this swimsuit on. For now I'll set it aside and see if by summer I feel confident enough to go swimming it or not. Love the color and style, just isn't for me at this current time.

These are gorgeous swim cover-ups, as long as you do not dry them in the dryer (and probably a good idea to wash on cold/delicate cycle). However, after returning from our Cancun trip I (as always) did laundry and included this beautiful turquoise one in my colors load. I washed on cold as I always do, and dried on med-low, as I always do. Big mistake. In the pic you can see just how much it shrunk - at least 3 inches in length and probably a good 2 inches in width. These are both size L. I did not take the black one on my trip so it has never been washed. Before washing these fit very comfortably with about 2 inches of extra room around the waist when tied completely closed. Now, the turquoise one is super tight to close it completely and the length is very awkward. Also, since the length has shrunk, the “waist” now hits *right* where the underwire of my bra is (empire waist style), and it is very uncomfortable. Going to give it away to my 5’ tall niece (I am 5’8”, 177 lbs, 36DD, and wear a size 10).

I am 70 years old, weight is 136 and I’m 5’5. I got a medium and it fits great. occasionally the pant leg will rise when I’m walking. It’s fully lined. I like the length of the top and the little gathers on the side of it. It seems to be well-made.

I wanted a new bathing suit and when I saw this, I liked the design right away even though I've never had a skirt suit before. I'm 55 and even though I'm 5'10" and about 118lbs, I'm not in the best shape and have flabby thighs. I thought this would be perfect as it didn't look dowdy or old fashioned. I got it in size Small and it fits well and holds me in, but I'm long in the torso and it feels a little short waisted to me. The skirt is shorter than I expected, too. Not dealbreakers by any means, it still fits and looks fine.I especially like the colors. The black tummy control bottom hides a multitude of sins and the pretty blue top and little cutout in the middle draws the eye upward. It's an attractive style that is designed to flatter and it works. At the time of this review, it had a 15% off coupon making it an great value.

I'm taking swimming lessons and I'm usually very cold in the pool so I wanted a long sleeve option. This is 3 separate pieces, shorts, tank top and the jacket. I like the bright blue color. I'm only 5 feet tall and around 105 lbs, my normal size is x-small but small was the smallest option. This fits a bit loose on me and it gets looser once I'm in the water. I do like how modest it is especially since I'm using this for my swimming classes.

I'm 5'6", 185lbs, long torso. I ordered xl and like very much how it fits me, and camaflouges imperfections on my elder upper thighs. I don't have to wear long shorts under my top now. I ordered another one.

Beautiful teal color. I like the ruched sides, they seem to be more flattering than straight seams. I am 5'6" and 198 lbs., bra size 38B. I ordered the XL. You can see in the photos, it is snug but not overly so. I haven't gone swimming in it yet but seems like it will stay put and not be floating up around me while wearing it. I like that it is two parts, especially for when its time to use the bathroom. The bottom piece comes just below my belly button and is a skirt with 'pants' underneath. The construction of the suit is good. The bra in the top does want to ride up on me while wearing it, revealing the white bra that it is. The foam cups are adjustable so moving them around can solve that. I'm looking forward to summer and trying this out!

This set was okay for me. I struggle with an overall size for two piece swimsuits as my bottom is usually one size larger than my top. That being said, if you are a more universal size on the top and bottom, this might be a good set for you. The quality is actually pretty nice — the garment is constructed well and it washes up nice in a cold wash with an air dry. I have not had an opportunity to wear enough yet to see if the color fades or if the fabric stretches out over time, but for now, those aspects are good. For reference, I am 5’11”, 180lbs.

Nice, athletic, modest fit for large busted, apple shaped women! I was looking for something cute I could run around with my kids on vacation in that wasn't too boobalicious and not old lady-ish. This fits the bill! I'm 5 feet tall, 167 lbs, 40D bust and the 16 plus size fits perfectly. I originally ordered an 18 plus going by the measurements listed, but that was too big. I haven't worn it swimming yet, but initial fit is perfect. No underwire in the bra top, but I find it supportive enough without. Nice, cute suit!

I was a little skeptical but I always am when ordering something I can't try on. But omg I am obsessed with this swimsuit! It's very comfortable and not overly tight. I adore the print and colors. I will probably end up ordering more. I'm 5'1 209lbs and a 42D and ordered an 18, for reference.

So I have spent days and days going though swimsuits to find one to find my body. I'm 5'4", weigh 200lbs and carry most of it in my 40DDD's and belly. I have returned so many suits because they didn't have any kind of breast support. Yes, smaller sized women say there is support but when you have older saggy big breast, there isn't any support. I'm not going to tell you that it was excessively supportive, but it fit about like one of my old worn-out bras, which is still way more then I've found in any other suit. Since it is a bra and fits more like a bra, it works much better the others. The tank that you can wear over it covers all the rolls you want to hide. The tank is made out of the same swimsuit material and looks great. I wore it today to the lake for over 5 hours and always felt covered and comfortable with other people seeing me in it. The only downside to this suit is that the shorts fit like shorts. On my fat thighs, I have problems with shorts this length rolling up when I sit down or the seams chafing when walking. I will probably wear it with my other bottoms that are board shorts. The colors on this is so darn stinking cute and I hate to give up these shorts. I doubt I'll ever take off the tank around other people, but will at home around only my family. I'm so glad that I tried this. I never even thought about a 3 piece suit before and it is WONDERFUL! Give it a try, especially if you're large chested and having a hard time with swim suits.

I ordered according to the size-chart and it fits perfectly. I wear a size 22 dress, but ordered a 26 in this suit. The bust is not too large-although I have a B/C cup bust. The shorts are king enough to cover the chub where my legs meet, and the over piece camouflages my middle-aged belly. I’m so pleased! I can’t wait to wear it to deep water exercise class! I hope it stands up to the chlorine well.

Y'all, this one was my FAVE purchase for our beach vacation! I read the reviews, and though it was TTS to a little small, I went ahead and sized up twice. I wear a 20-22 in jeans (18 in Judy Blues), and a 2XL in tops and 3X in tees (because I like things loose). I'm 5'5 and a 44DDD / F. I went with the size 26. The bottoms are a bit too low cut for this 69 year old, so I pair the top with a different more full coverage black bottom that I used with my last suit. I’m happy with this purchase and the top isn’t too blousy like some of these types of suits can be. Update: after wearing this suit several times, I can say that the cup inserts in the top are a little on the smaller size. If you are a bigger breasted woman, you might want to separately order some larger cup inserts to replace with the ones that the top comes with. There is adequate room in the top for a larger cup.

BUY THIS SWIM SUIT! I LOVE mine. I haven’t bought a swimsuit in a decade. At my last family reunion, I went kayaking in yoga pants. I decided to take the plunge this time, and order a suit on-line. After reading reviews for days, I settled on this, and boy am I glad! PROS: I have a long torso. This suit fit very well, didn’t ride up my booty. Suit give good bust support, which I need. My shape is really weird 36D bust 30” waist 35” hips 5’7”, 120 to 125 lb Age: senior citizen/granny Suits that fit bust are usually too loose in legs. This style is very accommodating. I suspect it would be flexible for many shapes. I am older than dirt, and wanted modest coverage, but still wanted to be ‘cute’. Okay, ignore my wrinkles and pasty complexion. This suit makes me feel attractive and my DH gave it two thumbs up. The gathering top and flowing skirt give me the appearance of a waist AND, Cover my tummy and mort importantly my not so great booty. CONS I have to read up on how to set the colors, so they don’t fade in chlorine. Several people said colors ran or faded. I want to avoid that The boy shorts underneath are loose/baggy, but the skirt covers that. Narrow hips are hard to fit. I may update this review after actually wearing it in water.

This tankini has its good bits and bad bits. First the GOOD: POCKETS!! The shorts have pockets and they are cut loosely at the hem so they look more like regular shorts. The shorts have a bikini bottom built in similar to the shorts you see within a skort. The color is pretty and I like the look of a patterned top with black bottoms. The not so good: the built in bra is not supportive. The straps are extremely stretchy so they don't offer a lot of support and the built in bra is not quite long enough to cover my bust - the elastic doesn't touch my rib at all in the front. The bra's elastic band is about 1 1/2" too long, so things could slip out over time. The built in bra is also rather short so I either show a lot above or have a lot hanging out below it. Neither of which is particularly comfortable. The tiny pads drift around in the front, but stay mostly in the center where they belong. The padding should be much larger as well as the shelf bra. The seam along the top is slightly lower than the bottom of the shelf bra, but still falls a bit high. This may offer some support for smaller chested women, but given that the average American woman is a 36DD, I'm still surprised that it's extremely rare to find bathing suits built for larger busted women.

I'm 5' 7" 170lbs and bought size medium. I took "un-posed" as possible pics so you can actually see what it looks like on a person just existing- not trying to look a certain way. Pros: This suit has BEAUTIFUL, vibrant colors and a lovely pattern. Flattering design- love the ruffles on the shoulders. Love that the back is a hook, not strings. Cons: The bottoms definitely don't have "tummy control" and also don't quite fit how I had hoped.. the band is kinda wide in my opinion and would be more flattering if the hip was cut higher on the leg. The bottoms are a little tight while the top is a little loose. I wish i could tighten the straps more- they came all the way tightened all the way and are too dang loose (and I'm pretty normal when I comes to sizing usually). The bra pads are almost too small for the bikini and they're kinda flimsy. Summary: This bikini is super adorable, good for the price, decently comfortable, and does the job. Don't expect anything super fancy, but this is great for just a normal- ass bikini to wear to the pool or whatever. You probably won't feel like a sexy goddess, but seriously, it's summer. Don't worry too much about how you look, just go have fun 💓

Perfect bikini. Honestly it is the bikini I grab instantly to feel confident. The colors are vibrant, the fit is comfortable, the style is flattering. I am 5’5 and around 165-170 pounds. I order a medium but I love a tight and skimpier look. So if you want more coverage you might want a large. The bottoms are adjustable and the top too. There are a lot of heavy chested women on here looking for a bikini with support for their breast. I am a 34DD and I find that if you wear the top a little differently you can get a whole lot more support from this top than the way it is sold. I do this by removing 2 of the straps and towing them around my neck the way I would with any other triangle bikini. And then leaving the other two straps the way that they came. I adjust the straps around my shoulders how I would a bra. The adjustable straps aren’t cheap so they wont slip after you adjust them. I just think this bikini is worth the money for sure. I will be ordering in every color if I can.

It fits great! There are a few things that could have been done better to make it look a little nicer, like where the straps are attached, but all in all I think it's a great suit for the price. I will say, I'm a 32A &, I don't see this top fitting larger cup sizes.

This is a very cute suit. It feels like a more modest option which I appreciate on days when I feel like hiding my post-partum tummy a bit. It does a good job of camoflauging parts I might not feel like highlighting most days. This suit is truly adorable while being attractive and still age-appropriate for us grown folks (Please don't take this the wrong way but int he spirit is is intended - By this I mean not looking like a larger version of a toddler's one-piece, NOT that we have to tone it down and hide or some such nonsense as we get older). I love the little skirt. It is sewn onto the body and is not removable. This is what I expected from the ad but I wish it was only sewn onto the hips so that I had the option to tighten or loosen the skirt by tying it like a wrap-around dress. This is a super small complaint, though. Overall this is beautiful and flattering.The skirt hits just under the swell of my hip, not too cheeky, just right, super flattering. I'm 5'4" for reference and the lowest part of the front of the skirt hits 4" under my bellybutton. I am 160lbs, 38D/ 43" bust and 34.5" waist and 44.5" hips and I picked the size XL and it fits very nicely, a bit loose but supportive enough. The bottoms under the skirt are connected tot he one-piece as well of course and have high french-cut leg openings. The skirt covers most of this, like 905 of my bum in these. It shows just enough to be flirty without having my cheeks out which is cool for some days, like for example my nieces swim party where I don't want to wrestle with a cover-up all day and not want to take it off even though I feel like swimming because I would feel a tiny bit self-concious in one of my bikinis around my extended family and their kids (It shouldn't really matter and it's not a big deal I just mean it's nice to have more covered options on some occasions, you know?.). This suit is a vibrant color just as pictured. A beautiful rose red. It is lined and is comfortable. It is true to size. i did measure it and it matches the provided chart. It has removable very flat round nipple-cover-type pads in the bust area. You can simply pull them out (or add larger ones) if you want. I like the nice thick supportive straps and the metal hardware that will last without chipping and breaking like plastic can. I like that it isn't overly padded too. It has no underwire. The fabric is thick and feels like quality material.There were a few small areas that had loose stitching on the shoulder ties but I usedmy sewing machine to fix these. They were tiny and I didn't mind fixing them but I wanted to mention them because no one else should have to sew a brand-new suit. This may be something that rarely happens with its company's products, Idk, but that is why I took off a star.

I’m a bit disappointed with this swimsuit because the listing images and reality are not the same. In the listing images, the colors look so bright and almost neon. However, in person the colors are a lot more dull and muted. Also, this suit is very difficult to get into without help. In order to get the straps over my head to do the crisscross in the back, I have to loosen the straps up all the way. Well, once the straps are on, then it is quite difficult to adjust them back. I need an extra set of hands with this suit. Let’s just say, it would be awful if I was at a public pool in this suit and had to use the bathroom. What a hassle. Other than those issues, everything else was fine. The fit is true. I’m 5’6, 150#, 34D, and normally a size medium in one-piece swimsuits. I ordered the medium in this suit and it fits, I might have also fit a large. The size chart was pretty wishy-washy about the bust size and cup size. My size fell into two different sizes, so I just went with what I know. Overall, a cute swimsuit but just not what I expected in more ways than one.

I do not often write a review, but had to share how great this coverup is! It is soft, lightweight and feels great against my skin (makes we want to sleep in it!). I am tall and have long legs, and coverups often barely cover everything. This coverup is long enough to fully cover my backside. I will be comfortable going from beach/pool to a restaurant at a resort wearing this. The sleeves are loose and flowy, very comfortable.I machine washed it using a lingerie bag and hung it to dry. The tag said NOT to machine dry it. Fabric is rayon, which should never be dried (which is why some reviewers experienced major shrinkage I think). Being rayon, it dried with wrinkles. I ordered the blue tye dye, so they are not really noticeable. If wearing as a dress I would iron it. I think the wrinkles would definitely be more noticeable on the solid fabrics.I am 5'9." My measurements are 43" (DD), 40", 47." According to size chart I could have ordered XXL, but after reading reviews, I decided to order the XL, and I am 100% pleased with the fit. If you do not mind line drying your coverup, I highly recommend this item!

Overall I love this bathing suit. For the most part it's flattering due to the design/ colors but doesn't hide the belly fat from every angle as you can see. I'm in the process of loosing weight so hopefully I can loose enough weight to enjoy this bathing suit although I am considering a return if I can't shed enough weight within a month or so. Otherwise I highly recommend this bathing suit.

I ordered a couple of sizes and one other swim dress from a different brand/ seller, but this is the CLEAR winner! 1. It makes me feel GREAT! SO cute! 2. It's NOT A ONE-PIECE! I hate wearing one-piece swimsuits because logistically trying to get them off, and especially back on (ahem, restroom visit), while they are wet is literally the worst. This is essentially a dress tankini , so whatever bottoms you like best, you can wear those! I have a high waisted set from Torrid that I love, and I'll be wearing those with it most likely. 2. Length is that just-right-still-short-enough-to-be-flirty sundress vibe! 3. Shape - bodice crossover design and waist yoke with ruching will make you look like an hourglass! 4. Straps are wide - super silky and comfy, and they don't take bites out of your shoulders - PLUS, better support! 5. Color is true to the photo - I love purple, and this is a nice blend of a deep violet, lilac, periwinkle, and lavender with some black, grey, and white in a floral that doesn't look dated. 6. Skirt has plenty of ease! I bought the 16 and 18 (I'm 5'56", ~200lb, and I'm at least one size larger from the waist down). I wound up going with the 16, because the skirt had plenty of ease to accommodate my larger hips, and the 18 was not as supportive in the bust. OVERALL, well made, comfy, and a delight to wear. I didn't want to take it off, so I've been wearing it around the house ALL day. If you've been thinking about it, just order one and send it back if you don't like it!

I ordered this top to wear as multi-purpose travel clothing, for the occasional swim or beach visit, but also to have in my warm weather appropriate wardrobe to wear as a tunic length sleeveless top.For reference, I have a full bust measurement of 44 inches, I wear a 34F bra but have am a size 8 from the waist down. According to the size chart with my bust measurement my best fit was XXL, but I knew that wouldn’t fit. The XL shelf bra is a bit short and offers no support, but it does include modesty pads (they are NOT support cups) for nipple coverage which is a nice benefit in a swim top. I’ll have to add length to the shelf bra and likely will also add 2” elastic banding to the bottom in lieu of the current 1” elastic. The entire front of the swim suit has at least 2 layers of fabric, the back has only one except for the band for the shelf bra. I will only wear this top over a wired bra or swim bra for support.The XL skims my torso and flares out a bit in the last 2 or so inches before the hem, which hits me at the low hip. The ruching is cute both for wearing as a swim top and also when I wear it as a sleeveless tunic, but it makes the front of the top droop lower than the back, despite my bust size.There isn’t anything that would be considered shaping or “tummy control” fabric or construction in the suit or liner. The lining is made entirely of lightweight quick dry stretch fabric so it feels nice and soft against the skin and I imagine it will perform well as swimwear because the lining doesn’t hold water. The outer fabric is quite a nice weight, I’d call it mid-weight nylon spandex and it is considerably heavier fabric than is often used for swimwear. This is a benefit because it will be less clingy due to the weight of the fabric. There is no additional banding or compression fabric between the outside fabric and the liner fabric, so I am not sure why this is called tummy control.

The “BMJL Women's Swimsuit Coverup Swim Beach Cover up Swimwear Lace Bathing Suit Cover ups Dresses” arrived promptly and was lovelier than described online. The beautiful white tiered fabric with intricate bodice lacework really stands out when viewed up close. All eyes are sure to turn in your direction when you set foot on the sand or appear at the pool wearing this airy and lightweight beach coverup! I ordered size “XL” for my 5’8” height, 160# and 38B. The strikingly feminine coverup is 34” in length when measured from the back neck down to the hem. The sleeves are 18-1/2” in length, falling with one gentle ruffle to below the elbow. Three tiers of gently gathered ruffles cascade under the bodice. There is a 6-1/2” neck opening in the front bodice for over-the-head easy on / easy off. A lovely 22” tassel tie offers optional closure at the round neckline. Excellent materials and superb craftsmanship are found throughout this loose-fitting, cool and airy garment. Fabric is 95% Polyester for durability and 5% Spandex for very slight elasticity. All seams are encased, with no loose threads. The high quality lace insert is strong and sturdy, yet remains delicate in appearance. There is great attention to detail wherever you look. This is obviously a garment that was made with high standards and pride. As a designer and seamstress of bridal gowns, I am very impressed with the excellent quality of this cover-up. Like all fine clothing, hand washing and air drying is recommended. I might fluff this garment in the dryer on “air only” for a minute or two as well and immediately hang to dry. This well-made garment is an excellent value for the money. I believe you will be very pleased, as I am, with your choice of adding this lovely BMJL cover-up to your resort / cruise-wear, summer or vacation wardrobe. Enjoy! Highly Recommended :)

I ordered another color of this suit back in May 2022 in the Aqua Navy printed. I mainly got it because I have a new grandbaby and wanted a suit that I didn't have to keep adjusting while trying to wrangle a newborn. The suit is well made, thick, nice quality, and lined. Score! I did not have the riding up issues that others mentioned and I actually liked the sewn in bra because again I didn't want something that had to be readjusted all the time. The sewn in bra was a rectangle shape like a traditional bra shape. The first one I bought was a size large. I am 5'3 and 128lbs and it fit fine, was not tight but I knew it would and it did get looser when wet. I bought another one because the first one worked out so well but ordered a medium this time to account for the looser when wet fit. It arrived today and it is still well made, thick, nice quality, and lined BUT there is a change. The padding for the bra is no longer sown in and can be removed. So it is no longer "sewn-in soft bra padding and elastic chest band provide more support" as stated in the description or like the one I bought in May 2022. Which also means it will constantly be moving around when you are moving around, swimming or playing with little ones and have to be adjusted repeatedly. As soon as I tried it on I had to adjust the padding in the bra which are 2 circle shapes, not triangular. They are really more to cover any area that you wouldn't want poking out, not for support, which detracts from the whole purpose of having a sewn in bra. Now it is only a built in padding not a built in bra which is something that I like and something I didn't have to constantly keep adjusting when wet or moving or fix after it comes out of the wash. I still give it 5 stars for quality, fits well and the suits being well made I just wish they had left the bra part alone.

The suit holds in place in the water and doesn't stretch, which I find some bathing suits do when weighed down by the water. I have not had any tearing issues and overall feel very comfortable when swimming in this. I hate the typical bathing suits because I always feel like it rides up my girl parts. However, with the boylegs, I do not have that issue and it gives enough coverage so I'm also not worried about my butt cheeks hanging out. When I get home from swimming, I immediately wash my suit in the washing machine, and I hang dry it. I do not use the dryer in fear it will ruin my suit. Overall, I love this one piece and will most likely purchase another one in a different color.**Edit 07/23/2021**I've had this bathing suit for more than a month now and it's still in great condition. I use this bathing suite on average, 4 times a week, doing one mile(1760 yards) freestyle laps for about 35-40 mins each session. Still there are no tears, stretching, or color fading. Again, I always wash the suite after every swim on gentle cycle in the washer and hang dry only. Great purchase!

Like many other reviewers I've been looking for a swimsuit that I feel cute in but isn't too revealing- when at the beach with my two young kids I don't want to feel too exposed. This fits the bill and seems well-made, though I haven't gotten it wet yet. I appreciated other reviewers posting pictures and sharing their size so thought I'd do the same. I'm a little under 5'7" and I'm 125 pounds, and pretty flat-chested. Many reviews had me thinking I'd be in the size small camp, but I also have a long torso and some recommended sizing up for that. And I was undecided on colors, so I got a medium in blue floral and a small in yellow to try on. Overall the difference in size is minimal. The small is a little more clingy, but the medium doesn't feel like it's going to fall off by any means. The biggest difference is in the seat of the swimsuit. Neither one feel like they're riding up or choking off my, er, lady parts, even with the extra layer of underwear for trying on. That's a miracle for someone with a long torso. But the small did seem to be squishing into my butt cheeks a little more. It's not that it's too short length-wise, but more like narrower from front to back, without much give. It wasn't so much as to be uncomfortable, but it was more noticeable visually- it was creating a little valley at the edge of the swimsuit into my butt cheek, if that makes sense. That made me a little more self-conscious, plus I find the blue floral color and pattern a little more flattering overall, so I'm sticking with a medium. If your body has a little more up top than below a small might be fine, though the bust area in both felt pretty good- I didn't feel like there was some huge gap between my actual chest and the fabric of the swimsuit, which is also nice. If someone's bigger on top the lacing in the back could be loosened up to provide more room, but the seat of the swimsuit just is what it is, so rounder bottoms might want to aim for a medium. :)

Like many women, i HATE bathing suit season but love being by the water. I gave birth to my son a year ago and have been DREADING shopping for new swimwear, especially after being through the pandemic and putting a few extra pounds on!! I found this swimsuit while looking for tankinis, and was in love with how unique and chic it looked on everyone in the reviews. Ladies, JUST BUY IT ALREADY! You could pay $150 for a comparable one at Nordstrom, or spend $30 and have more money to spend on accessories!!! It is sooooo flattering on my new mom-bod. This highlights all the right places and disguises the bad ones! The thought of a sheer middle made me uneasy BUT it actually creates a pretty awesome optical illusion and makes your waist look smaller! The material is super thick and soft. For reference, I am short and curvy: 5’0”, 155lbs, 36D and got the black in 10-12. Fits like a glove and the straps aren’t too big or loose. Also has great butt coverage and leg openings. Paired with a flowy kimono (also got the one i’m wearing on amazon) it’s my new go-to outfit for hanging out by the water with my toddler!! It washes great if done on a cool/gentle setting, then hung dry. Because of the mesh pieces I want to be careful not to warp them in the dryer. My only issue is that the criss-cross on top is pretty big and makes a funny tan line! You can lower the straps and push them off your shoulders for a sleeveless suit. Hope this helps! 😉

I've never order a swim suit online before, so I ordered three suits from different brands and this is the one I am keeping. For starters, the quality of the material is top notch, the colors are vibrant, and the entire suit is lined. The material is thick but stretches nicely. I noticed there were some complaints about the torso of this suit being too short, but that was not an issue for me. I'm 5'7", weigh 150 lbs., and I ordered a large. The only area of the suit that didn't fit perfectly was the chest area, but I'm a B cup so I figured a size large might be too big in the chest. However, it wasn't too big to the point it looks bad or feel uncomfortable. Everything feels "tuck in" and the suit has nice bottom coverage. It's a great suit for moms that don't want to be falling out everywhere when playing in pool or on the beach with their kids. The style is super flattering and my kids told me I looked pretty the first time I wore it. I highly recommend this suit!!

I have a toddler so if I'm at beach or pool I'm up and down, running around like a chicken with its head cut off. I'm a lady who wants every inch of cheek fully covered without having to constantly pull it down. I'm 5'2 135-140lbs post baby and got a 10 (6 was recommended). If I hadn't sized up, the torso would not have been long enough- and really isn't even now. I'd love the band to hit just below my boobs but it is a couple inches below...I can only imagine how low it would be on a smaller size. I obtained the full cheek coverage I desired, at the cost of the top cups being too large. I'm a 32c and breastfeeding so my boobs are heavy and I wish it had more support. Yet it looks beautiful on and I can nurse in it, and the butt stays in place. So I'm still on the fence whether to keep it. In bikinis I get a small, tight top for support and a larger bottom for coverage. It's hard with a one piece to choose which area to sacrifice...an uncomfortable choice no matter what. Last summer I had support but had to wear short to cover my rear. Now I'll have rear coverage but the pain of saggy boobs whipping around. Ugh IDK.

I LOVE my blue one of these. It has a Hilor label, and is size 10. (Even though I'm a 4-6 in real life, 5'8, 140.) It is beautiful and covers me well but still feels pretty. So I ordered a black one which was sent with NO tags, no Hilor label, and said it's a size L (not even an option - I ordered another 10!). Most bizarrely, it was sent from an unnamed sender in Kansas City. It fits terribly, has white cups inside (which don't blend in with the black), and is poorly sewn. Just reordered and this one is beautiful - cups are black, fabric is a richer black, ruching is more pronounced (and hides belly better), and stitching and macrame are better and stronger. Even the boob cups overlap more/provide better hold and coverage at front. Definitely return if you don't get one with a Hilor tag and a numerical size - these other ones are awful!

I wear a 34DDD, so it can be really difficult to find good fitting bathing suits. I'm 5'9 and 143 lb. I am so thrilled about how flattering this bathing suit is and the fact that it flatters my body type. I'm probably going to try to get another one in a different color as well. The swimsuit quality and stitching is top notch. Absolutely no complaints!

That being said, I wanted a bathing suit to cover and camouflage the damage (weight gain), remaining covered while chasing & swimming with my toddler, plus look/feel good in it & hopefully even sexy. This mostly fit the bill, while the strappy back design seems cute, it’s not the easiest to get properly into place & can make back fat a little bit noticeable, the lower back pinched in a little as well. Think I’m possibly returning it, as the back straps being a pain to manage to get right & not entirely flattering as well as the lower back area pinching my fat a bit too. Still wish the back straps didn’t require so much fooling around with to make it look as is intended. I added updated pics for comparisons sake, it should be the last 3pics.

As a middle aged lady who, like many of us, is carrying a few more pounds than she would prefer, I’m a little self conscious about wearing a bathing suit. I really want to be able to enjoy the beach and pool, though, so I’ve been searching for a new suit that would give me coverage I like and that I will feel comfortable wearing in public - and this one hits the mark! I’ll describe what I like, from top to bottom: The wider straps help provide more support and don’t cut into my shoulders. The higher back coverage makes my whole torso look a little slimmer and feels supportive (holds in a couple lumps and bumps). The chest has good support and isn’t low cut. The ladies are able to stay in place without being smushed together or bulging out the top. The ruching across the mid section camouflages my stomach. The print also provides camouflage (I don’t think I would like this suit in a solid color). The skirt skims over my hips. It’s not clingy at all, so it also covers my less than toned thighs. The “shorts” are wonderful under the skirt, as they truly do give upper thigh support. Overall, I think this a GREAT suit, and the price is on point. For reference, I’m between 5’4” and 5’5” and currently weigh about 188. Happy Shopping!

The underside of the skirt is shorts so you dont need to worry about bikini line which is great for my sensitive skin since I get razor burn easily. I ordered the cherry print size 12 (I've gain quite a bit of weight lately and have gone from an 8 to 12 in Jean's so i got the 12)... the 12 fit a little loosely, and would be much too loose once wet. I definitely recommend riding a size down. My boyfriend doesnt like it because he says it looks like a dress not a swim suit and hides my butt lol The cherry print design looks a little too busy on so I ordered me the navy polka dot one in the 8-10 size. Also, the first suit came over night free with prime, the second one i ordered tues will be here in 2 days which is great for the last minute pool function we got invited to for saturday. Super excited I was able to get so quickly Overall, good product just buy a size down.

I'm 5'6", 200 lbs, and something like a 36DD. This bikini top fits me quite nicely and provides a little more coverage than typical slinky tops, so I feel like it's safer for around family, or the neighbors, etc. Also, I appreciate that it's not yet another halter style top so my neck isn't doing all the work to hold up the weight of my ta-tas. It does have an underwire, which I usually avoid, but this wasn't at all uncomfortable. And the padding is minimal, which is more my style. This is NOT any sort of push up top, which is fine by me since I'm not interested in an inch of extra padding, but just something to note. The extra fabric below the normal bra band is something to be aware of since if you're more curvy like me that could potentially end up riding up. But so far, it's a cute top and I'm liking it. I might even also play around with re-stringing the back ties so that the straps cross in the back and then connect to the opposite side since I think that will provide some better security with holding the shoulder straps up so that they aren't sliding down my shoulders if I'm in the ocean battling waves. Ultimate result: I'm happy and really like it!

At the bottom of the line section it fit perfectly at my natural waist so I think that the suit must be around 34 inches an inch or two above the belly button. I never feel comfortable in swimsuits. They are never flattering and always either make me feel like a stuffed sausage, a granny, or like my girls will pop out at any moment. I'm mid weight-loss and didn't want to spend a ton of money on a new suit for vacation where it would only likely be used once, but needed something so bought a few and hoped for one that at least fit, even if it wasn't flattering. I'm 5'4", about 200lbs, and hold the majority of my weight in my stomach. Typically wear a 2xl top and 16 bottom. Ended up buying size 18. Well, this is the first swimsuit I've worn in probably 20 years that I actually feel comfortable in. It's not just plain black or covered in flowers, it isn't putting my rolls on display, but it's also not totally conservative either. The girls aren't in danger of flopping out, but the mesh slit in the front is a nice peek-a-boo. The ruching on the lower half helps hide my stomach, while the two tone color draws attention up and away from my stomach as well. I feel like it flatters me pretty well. The top is ever so slightly too big for me, but if I went down a size the bottom half would be too tight. For the price, it also feels pretty well made. I wish it had slightly better support for the girls, but at least they don't droop out to each side and look that way like so many other suits. Definitely recommend this swimsuit, especially if you're apple shaped and struggle to find things like me.

I ordered size 16 first and it would’ve worked but seemed too short and I’m only 5’3” and 200lbs with momma pouch; which this suit does well for that! I reordered and got size 18 and fits better. It’s a little loose for me in the breast area but I’ve lost weight in that area but still looks and fits just fine! The only thing I would change is it’s kinda tight on the thighs.

Loved this suit!! It was so extremely flattering and I’m not a small girl. It held the girls nicely. It didn’t have overly tight elastic around the thighs, therefore it didn’t cause a roll or line in my thigh flub. I bought it in the black and white stripes and the flowers with palm leaves. They both looked great. If you are worried about tan lines with a higher cut suit and the mesh cutout, don’t it is easily able to be unhooked and tucked in for sunbathing. I ordered this in a 2XL or 18W and it fit perfect. I am holding a lot of my whopping 68lb pregnancy weight so I don’t know my exact weight. I’m currently in either a 16 or 18 in jeans. I’m at least 210lb and I have a D-DD sized cups for reference. Overa definitely recommend and aqua Eve has other suits that are also awesome! You won’t be disappointed.

So flattering and incredibly comfortable! Even supportive for my 38DDD (G) chest! I've never worn a 2-piece before, and I've certainly never put bathing suit pictures of myself onto any website/app. But being a thick woman, I want to give an honest review and honest pictures to help other thick women! I'm 5'2", 250, apple-shaped, and normally wear an 18 or 20. I initially ordered a 20 in this suit because of my chest size, but decided it was too large and then sent it back and ordered an 18 (returns are SUPER easy BTW). The 18 fits terrific!!! I do wonder if a 16 would be even more supportive for my chest though, so might order one and then update this post. I love the truly high-waisted bottoms as well as the adjustable straps on the top... as a short woman, I have a short length from my shoulders to my chest and always need to shorten straps as far as possible, and this top allows for that! The straps are also removable, in the fact that you can make them cross in the back or just go straight down. In the first picture, the straps are crossed in the back, the second picture they're going straight down - crossing them shortens the straps a little and adds more support for us large-chested ladies. All in all, I'm SUPER HAPPY with this purchase and how confident it makes me feel! And for only $40 this is a steal!!! UPDATE: I've worn it roughly 20 times, and it fits just like the first wear - holds up well, no stretching!

Update: Still LOVE this suit! It's 7+ months later, and a full summer of boating & swimming has gone by. And the suit held up well, and still fits well, even as I'velost 110+ lbs. Edges are still fine. Colors are still bright. Suit is extremely comfortable to wear all day on the water. I'll be ordering a new one soon in a different style for next summer season. Just came in today. Super cute & fits great! It's the 5th suit I've bought this season, and first one I'll be able to keep. Yeah! I'm 5ft 2 in, & weigh 203#. I wear size 16 regulars & XL in clothes, but always buy a size 18 bathing suit. After returning 3 others (and not able to fit comfortably in the 4th suit after washing), I decided to try this 18W. My measurements are 46 bust, 43 waist, 46 hips, so I was worried the waist might be a bit snug since the sizing chart says it's a 39.5 inch waist, and I would need a 20W for me. But my bust & hips were middle of the range for the 18W. The flowing A-style design flows down from just under the bust & flows over the waist & belly. Shorts are well-fitting too, with plenty of coverage all around & not tight at all. The back is traditional straight line, not too high for me. Straps are medium width, and the girls are well-supported. It's a printed design (on one side only), printed onto white material. Colors are vibrant. The edge is NOT hemmed / sewed. I don't like that, but have struggled to find anything with a sewn hem. So decided to give this try after reading thousands of reviews. I may take it to my local tailor to see if she'll sew it for me. Until then, I'll see how well it holds up in the wash.
