Ordered 18 Plus. I am a 42B and usually have issues with tops being tight. Thus one fit well and was surprisingly flattering. Bottoms are a little tight in the band, but not terrible. Only thing I would really change is I wish the bottoms were a little thicker. They cause a little bit of camel toe action if I don't pull them down every once in a while. Overall I'm pretty satisfied.

I bought this suit to swim laps at my local pool and was extremely happy to see it has a CLOSED back with no opening at the middle of my back! I originally bought this suit and another boy short model that had that stupid mid back opening and returned it immediately because it was so uncomfortable- I could tell it definitely would have been an issue in the pool. Also, the other brand’s suit (that was new!) came dirty with white lines all over the black suit (maybe deodorant??) gross. This suit however came clean and fits GREAT because the back is fully covered except for at your shoulders with the slight criss cross material which is nice to add a bit of fun to the suit. It doesn’t inhibit my swimming at all while working out doing freestyle laps. I’ve worn this suit 3 times now since I got it this weekend and have hand washed it just as much—the color has held up beautifully. The tummy control feature is very subtle (meaning it’s not like spandex and it won’t feel tight while working out in the water, but it’s definitely there)! The straps are sturdy and the bikini fit at the bottom is very comfortable. I’m only 5 feet tall and 200 pounds, so a well fitting suit is VERY hard to find, especially one to workout in, but I’m extremely happy with this purchase! The stretch is perfect and the chest support is great- I never ever have to adjust it when I’m in the water and I don’t feel that it gets looser once I’m in the water, either. I also want to add that taking the cups out was easy and the suit isn’t too see through without them, either, which is important for such a bright color screaming “look at me”! Sorry for not including a pic of me actually in the suit, but I figured I should show the beautiful vibrant pink color is true to the listing photos! Very happy!!! 10/10 and will definitely be getting more for my swim workouts! :) thank you!!

Fit perfectly. I'm 5'1" 185lbs, 40DD and a total apple shape. I can never find swimsuits that don't squish, or make me feel like a sausage. This is perfect. It covers what I want covered, with graceful and flowing fabric in the front, so it doesn't feel like a mumu. I have a very small bottom, and it still fits perfectly. Comes down on my legs so nothing is exposed, but again, I'm not wearing a tent. Bust is supported, as well as any suit does, plenty of room, but not too much. I don't feel like anything is going to pop out, and it fits nicely on the shoulders. It doesn't make me look like a size 10, but I'm not ashamed to get in the pool. Quality of fabric and stitching both seem good. All in all, a good purchase. I'm quite happy with this!

I love this suit. Very cute. I get lots of compliments. The green is very vibrant and a true emerald. The bottoms are very forgiving. They hide a multitude of sins. Lol. The cut is very low on the legs, and there is plenty of coverage for your behind. Plus, the rouching camouflages that muffin top very well. I'm 5'5", and the waist can be pulled up almost to my chest if I want it to. Or you can slouch it down a little if you don't have a many rolls to conceal! And the top is great except for one thing. The wider straps are great, and you can even move them. They have hooks so you can uncross them if you prefer. The cup size is generous and has removable pads. However...You can't adjust the width of the band at all. My chest is only a 38, even though I wear a 16 in jeans. So the 18W fit perfectly on the bottom, and the cup size of the top fit my DDD chest just right, but the band is a little loose which makes the girls sag a little because I can't tighten the straps too much without the band riding up. Other than that, great suit! I may try taking in the sides a little if I get brave!

This bathing suit is super cute! I am normally an 18W, 2X, 42D bra. I have been on a weight loss journey, so I got my normal size of 18W, instead of sizing up to a 20W. The 18W fit perfectly! I was expecting it to be too tight because I normally have to size up in swimsuits, so I was pleasantly surprised. I could lose another 10lbs and it would still look great too. I don't have the confidence for a bikini, so the cutout lets me show a little skin without being a full out bikini. The tie in the center of the bust allows for some contour and makes the boobs look great. I am on the smaller chest size, so I think this swimsuit has great support and coverage. I'm not sure how it would be for someone with larger breasts. The clasp in the back allows for adjustment across the chest area so you can customize your fit a bit. The straps and attachments are all sewn well and look like they will be very durable. The inner part of the swimsuit is a mesh that really helps to sculpt and hold things in. I instantly felt thinner in this suit because it helped to smooth out my lumps, bumps, jiggles, and rolls. The color contrast on the top compared to the bottom helps to draw the eye upward, which is a benefit for me because my hips are my widest and biggest part. The back comes up high enough to provide good coverage and hid all of my back fat rolls. The legs are not high cut and i found that they provided great coverage and didn't let any hip or lower belly fat show. I think I have a normal length torso, so if you have a long torso, it might not be the same for you. I washed it on cold and hung to dry and it didn't fade or shrink at all. I'm looking forward to wearing this through the summer!

The bottoms are not high cut and cover my hips well so my lower belly fat and hip jiggles aren’t exposed at all. The ruching on the front is very attractive and hides imperfections well. The top comes down low enough across my hips to make it look like a one piece. Disappointing. Bummer.

This is the most comfortable swimsuit I’ve ever owned. I kinda wish it had a built-in bra, but I’ve been wearing it to daily water aerobics using either a regular lightly padded bralette underneath, which works perfectly fine and holds things together. The straps of the bra can’t be seen thanks to the swim shirt. The shorts are a good length - just above my knee (I’m 5’2”). The whole outfit looks well put together, and I hope the colors will stay as crisp as they are after just two weeks or wearing it daily. Oh, best of all - the top does not float up at all, no matter how much you jump or move around in the water. I’m beginning to eye the colors for a second swimsuit. The color has stayed true in spite of multiple uses. Note: I bought a second swimsuit, in Floral 19. The shirt with a zipper is not as comfortable as the shirts with no zipper. The zipper feels heavy and pulls down on the fabric. While the blue print is lovely, the blue shorts have become faded into several shades of blue/purple. The contrasting yarn at the seams all over the swimsuit turned into an intense red that clashes against the blue of the swimsuit. In summary, I really like and recommend the black swimsuit. I cannot recommend the blue swimsuit. Will lower the stars. .

I'm 5'6" and around 118 pounds. I ordered a size small and it fits really well. The cups are a tad too big, but that's how everything fits me up top (I'm a 30B or 32A for reference). It's not big enough to be a problem. This is the most flattering swimsuit I've ever owned. Even though I'm pretty thin, I do have some belly rolls that I'd rather not flaunt at the beach, and these bottoms have the perfect coverage. The ruching is really flattering. The straps on the top are adjustable and I had to make them pretty small to fit (again, 32A problems), but there's still plenty of tightening that can be done. The ties on the top can be tied a couple of different ways. I like them tied a little lower down on my torso to cover a little more skin, but you could also tie it higher, under the bustline for a more traditional bikini look. I'm seriously considering buying a second one in another color as a backup! It seems well-made. I didn't notice any loose threads or anything like that. The fabric also seemed opaque enough that it won't become transparent when wet. The fabric is nice and stretchy so if I gain weight (always a goal of mine!) I know it will still fit for a while. A lot of times, bikinis in my size are too skimpy for my taste. The bottoms don't even cover my bottom and the top is like two eyepatches on a string. My stats: 5’3, 130 lbs, 32DDDD, size 3/5 bottoms, small tops. I’m wearing a size 4. As someone one who owns three summersalt swimsuits, I can confidently say that this is a great alternative to their one shoulder design. The one thing I don’t particularly like about my summersalt swimsuits is the lack of cups. I’ve had three kids, I need the additional coverage that a cup provides or you can clearly see my nipple. That’s not really a look I’m going for when I’m swimming with my kids. I don’t have this issue with this swimsuit. While I prefer the look of the summersalt one shoulder design over this one, mainly because I’m not a huge fan of the bunched fabric look, I feel more confident in this swimsuit. I’ve had three kids, and after my last baby I just haven’t had as much time or energy to work out. I also have a bad habit of sitting on the couch eating trail mix after my kids go to bed, not a great combination. I’m still holding onto baby weight, so putting on a swimsuit isn’t one of my favorite activities. It’s important to me to feel confident when I’m in swimwear as I don’t want my kids to feel like I am uncomfortable in my body, and I don’t want to worry about how I look when I’m supposed to be enjoying time with my kids. This swimsuit makes me feel great about myself, my husband loves the way it looks, everything stays covered and this allows me to focus on having fun in the sun. Basically this swimsuit gives me everything I want in swimwear, confidence. While it’s a “hefty” price tag, it’s a quality item, and is in the similar notice range to summersalt.

This was such an unexpected surprise! I'm pretty big, about 250lbs and 5'2" with 46G breasts. I usually go to Lane Bryant or Torrid for swim wear. It really looked like I was wearing a dress instead of a swimsuit, and the little shorts kept my thighs from rubbing together (bonus). I ordered it in the black with white dots too (after vacation) and I love that one even more!

My sizing basics, to start: I am apple/heart-shaped (broad shoulders) Height 5'6" (170 cm) Weight 130 lbs (about 9 st.) The ruching was a little looser than I would have liked through my waist.

I have been searching for a simple one piece to avoid sunburn without having tons of extra fabric to hold water like most larger suits have. I'm 5'4" and about 360lbs and the 6X is clearly made for someone taller, but it fits fine. (I think it might even work for someone a bit heavier than me) I like that it comes up very high in the armpit so I'm less likely to get a sunburn on that area. It's just a plain, cheaply made bathing suit, but it's comfortable and functional. I do wish the gusset were wider, and I would prefer the leg holes be cut a bit lower, and the back higher, but it's not a deal breaker. I will say that the cups had just been folded in half and shoved in, so they're winked and they're difficult to get placed correctly. They're also thin, so you may want a dark color if you're worried about people noticing your nipples. There's also some uneven sewing and a bit of a weird spot where the bra shelf connects to the side. Overall, I'm just thrilled this suit exists in this size and I'm quite happy with it after one day in the pool.

I ordered a size large, and the bottoms fit great! I'm a big fan of high waist styles, and these come all the way up to my belly button. I am small chested (B cup), and the top is definitely a little big for me, but I can make it work if I tie it tight. I'm really happy with this, and you can never go wrong with black bottoms because you can mix with any bikini top!

WOW!! This bathing suit far, far exceeded my expectations. For reference, I am 5’9” and currently weigh about 165. I am still carting around about 25 lbs of pregnancy weight, and putting a swimsuit on gives me anxiety. Seeing pictures of me in one gives me more! But I don’t hate how this looks on me, even if I’m not feeling myself still. The fit is INCREDIBLE. I got a size large based on the size chart and other reviews. I am SO used to one pieces fitting bad b/c of my height. I usually have to order up a couple sizes so that the suit doesn’t ride up or fit terribly in the chest area, and even then it’s so often a hard miss. This suit fits like a glove!! I’ll let you judge how much it helps w the tummy (and I have a mom pooch). It certainly doesn’t make the stomach look bigger anyway. I think the coverage is fantastic as well. There’s also enough support for the ladies. My single complaint is that it doesn’t come in fun print or colors!!! I’d seriously buy like 5 more of these if they had cute prints. But I do love the brown. It’s a very flattering color and will look nice w a good fake tan this summer. I am telling you, look no further for your PERFECT suit 🤩. Now let’s bring on some summer already!!!

I like the pattern on this swimsuit. Everyone has a different body type so it's not expected that every swimsuit will be a winning fit. I ordered this for my daughter who has an apple shape. She's larger on top, has a thick waist, but no hips, and a small rear. The swimsuit fit well to accommodate the top of her body but was too big on bottom. She stands 5'1". The straps are adjustable so the length of the swimsuit was good for her torso. She didn't care for the bows on the shoulders but they can easily be removed by cutting a couple of stitches. Ultimately she could not wear this swimsuit because the leg openings are high cut and they gaped open around the top of her thighs and crotch. If she sized down then the top would be too tight.

SEE BELOW FOR WHY I DROPPED MY RATING IN LESS THAN 1 year First I have learned to always read reviews online, especially with clothing, suits,& bras. For two main reasons how it fits others with a similar body type and how the sizing runs. That said I am 5’1” 138lbs and my bra size is 32K (YES K! 😳) and I ordered a Large this suit fits very nice and of course my hubby loves it! It holds the girls in (although I did think the breast portion was moveable it is not so I showed two pics one with them more separated). I will say one person answered my question and I took their advice n went with what my body type is rather than bra size. There is a small amount of side boob as I call it but not to where there’s spillage. If I would have gone bigger the back would have been too large! UPDATED REVIEW! RATING DROPPED from 5 to 2! In less than one year this swim suit has lost ALL elasticity in the breasts and belly and is all see thru in the netted areas!!! I noticed this over time using it frequently while going into a hot tub. I also noticed black dots but not dirt when I would get in. Today I wiped my suit on my hand and sure enough it is LITERALLY FALLING APART. It will ruin our filter so on to a better quality suit. I may have expected a little more life than I should have given the price, but lesson learned. I hope this helps someone! Last two pics show how loose and the black stuff rubbing off into my hand!

I love old movies, and I especially like the sportswear styling of the 1940s. Because I appreciate retro style, I was drawn to the charming polka dot pattern and the overall design of the Yonique high-waisted bikini swimsuit. The nylon/spandex material is soft and comfortable, and the tummy control feature is subtle, which is great for hot weather. The top's clasp and neck ties provide a secure fit, and the removable pads are a nice touch, although I prefer to go without them. The back clasp has three options, but it is a little hard to hook. The skirt is flouncy and attached to a bikini bottom that ensures coverage. The modesty factor goes hand in hand with ‘40s retro! However, there were a couple of drawbacks that prevented me from fully embracing this swimsuit. Despite ordering a size large to accommodate my 5'8" height and 150lbs weight, the skirt was surprisingly long, making it impractical for swimming. It's more suited for lounging by the pool or tennis court with an adult beverage. Additionally, the ruching on the side, intended to adjust the length, proved fragile as one of the strings broke the second I tried to adjust it. This makes it essentially a skirt. Overall, while I appreciate the aesthetic and will still wear it for certain occasions, it's more of an outfit than a functional bathing suit due to the skirt's length and the fragility of the ruching strings.

This is an awesome swimsuit for anyone concerned with sun protection. I ordered this for long days in and on the water, and I couldn't be happier with the design. It isn't what I had expected, but I like it for water sports. Unlike a lot of one piece swimsuits, this one is actually easy to put on and take back off without dislocating a shoulder; I normally shy away from front zippers, but this one feels secure. The material is comfortably stretchy and supportive, and overall feels and looks high quality. I'm a middle aged woman who has recently gained a lot of weight, but I think this swimsuit is still flattering. For reference, I am 5 foot 7, weigh 170 pounds, have a 36C bust with narrow shoulders, a short torso as mentioned, and a wide but flat derriere, carry most of my weight in the middle, and wear a US women's size 10 or 30 pants (medium size pants and joggers at Old Navy and H&M), or a woman's 10 or 12 in dresses. I ordered this swimsuit in the "large" size, and the fit is perfect (see my pics). This is a comfortable, high quality swimsuit that offers great sun protection, and I would not hesitate to order again from this brand.

I love that the straps can be adjusted as well as the tie in the back. I think it gives good tummy coverage however it is not a suit with compression if that is what you are looking for. I would purchase this again and in other colors.

I'm still deciding if I like this on me or not. Seems like good quality, I love the colors. I ordered the blue striped size large. It fits my hips well and has good butt coverage, but the bust is definitely big for me, so it doesn't give much support/boost like the model photos. The cups are sewn in so I'm not sure if I'll be able to do anything about that. For reference I am 5'5, 180lbs, 36" bust, 32" waist, 45" hips, I carry most of my weight in my belly and thighs. My bust measured into a medium, waist large, and hips XXL. The strap length is adjustable and since it ties in the back you can tighten it as much as you want. Besides the cups being big the only thing I'm not sure about is the cutout. The rouched material on the belly tends to slide up since I have a round stomach, which makes the cutout look less rounded on the bottom and more straight (last pic). Overall I'd say I like it and it's good quality, just not sure the style is for me.

It’s so hard to find a swimsuit that is cute AND holds everything in place when you’re a mom of 3, especially when you have some extra jiggliness from losing a little bit of weight. Buying swimsuits online makes it even more difficult. So I personally appreciate seeing actual pictures of real people wearing items when trying to make decisions. That being said I’m 5’4”, 150 lbs, 34D, with a short waisted hourglass figure, wear size 10 in jeans, and generally a medium or sometimes large depending on the brand or style, but I tend to order swimsuits in a large. I really love this swimsuit! I ordered it in a large and it fits great. I like that the style is cute and a little different with the tummy cut out, but not too revealing. It’s a slate blue with a white top and black stripes. The suit itself is comfortable and I’m confident it will stay in place swimming, kayaking, and chasing my kids. It’s made of a nice quality fabric that is not thin or see through. It doesn’t suck you in per say, but it does feel a little supportive and the runching helps hide mommy tummy. The entire suit is lined, front and back. The top inserts are not removable and take up the whole busy area of the top. They are not awkwardly shaped or weird looking and you can’t see them through the fabric. The straps are adjustable and although there is no underwire, you are able to tie the back and help tighten the underbust area to provide a bit of support. Delivery was supposed to take a month (it came from China), but it actually arrived in about 2 weeks. I do really like this swimsuit and anticipate wearing it frequently on our upcoming cruise and into the summer. If you are thinking about getting this one I would highly recommend it because it’s a great quality swimsuit.

So I ordered the “halter” one piece swimsuit first and absolutely hated it. I decided to reorder the army green one piece with straps instead in an XL. I just had my first baby and wanted something that had nice coverage but was still sexy. As soon as I tried this on I fell in love with it!! I love the ruching to hide my belly and the tiny straps are very flattering. I personally like the v cut (I am a 38C) so it fit perfectly! The cups are a little odd but you can remove them or move them to fit your tatas! This is the 3rd swimsuit I’ve tried! Third time was a charm for me! I ordered this suit in 3 other colors because I love it that much! I highly recommend the one with regular straps not the halter.

It’s very flattering, snatches me in all the right places. Ordered for Memorial Day weekend will definitely be ordering in more colors, I’m normally not comfy in a bikini with my body but this really does the trick. Love it!

I am using this high quality one piece, boyshort legs suit for aquatic therapy. Specifically the type of therapy where you walk/run on a treadmill in pool water. I also do calesthetic exercises i.e. side leg lifts, squats, full body dips and kicks. I only tell you all this just you can see how rigorous a workout this suit can handle. The straps don’t move, the legs do not roll up and the chest area needs no adjustment. Although the legs look short on the suit by itself, when you put the suit on they actually are on my body 4 inches down from the top of my inner thigh. I have chosen to remove the extra padding for the chest area which the suit arrived with. I am apple shaped, and my legs are skinny by comparison to my waist. My waist does not curve in and consider myself straight up and down. The suit was easy enough to put on considering I have a bum right shoulder, osteoarthritis and a bad back. Ergo all the therapy. In getting out of the glass water tank I can simply pull the good arm out first and then roll the suit down and off! So easy! Btw I am 63 years old and removing a wet suit was key to my happiness with a suit. If you are a young lady in any shape this would be a really pretty, flattering suit to go to the beach or pool. Don’t let me forget for those who will be using this in the sun the material is UPF 50+. The photos I don’t believe do the suit justice. It is much thicker than it appears and the colors are more vibrant. I’ve attached a photo of the navy and I hope you can see the pretty blues. I would highly recommend.

My wife and I picked this beautifully colored swimsuit together. The boyleg style offers a little more coverage, so you automatically feel more at ease about your appearance. The sizing seemed small at first, but the material freely stretches to conform to your body. Even at a 36DDD, the swimsuit held up great and looked good while doing it. It was not too tight at all. We gave it a perfect 5-star rating overall, but we were a little disappointed at how unflattering the midsection was. Unless you're built like a supermodel, every detail of your midsection will be very visible. I think some tummy support bands would have gone a long way to make this swimsuit even more perfect. At just south of $30, this is a great buy. We highly recommend it.

To start off, I am 5'4" and I weigh around 155lbs - I got a large. I am not a super thin person but I'm also not overly curvy which may be important for optimal flattery. There's a few things I really like about this suit and a few I don't so I'll just do pros and cons. Pros: * The fabric is thick! There's two layers, one is the outer, tighter fabric. The inside is a softer, more meshy fabric that makes it a lot more comfortable. * I wore this thing twice in all and both times I had zero issues with wearing it for a long time. With a of of strappy suits you have an uncomfortable tightness or painful pulling on the strings (especially as a not so thin person) but I just didn't have that with this one. * Despite the fabric being thick and strong, it still dried very quickly after being in the sun. I hate soggy suits so this was a major win for me. * The tummy control it real. Obviously its just fabric and can only do so much, but it really is a relief not to have to worry about my mommy tummy saying hi to everyone too. * The back straps are really cute and come down pretty far so tan lines aren't as quirky. Cons: * This is on me but I did NOT like the color. I ordered the 'Cream Coffee' one thinking it would be darker than my skin (because I'm really pale) but its a lot lighter than the photos and ended up being more like a skin suit for me. * I love the idea of the retro, high hip cutout but they translated the thickness all the way around leaving a LOT of cheeky exposure. Had I been curvier, it may have been okay but with this color and the sort of bagginess it just looked bad. * When I first got the suit I wore it as is to the beach and washed it when I got home. After the wash I noticed that the stitching on the strap near the front is already coming unraveled. This actually alarms me because you don't want a tight, body control type item to have weak hems. Overall, I do like this suit. I ended up making some adjustments so I feel confident keeping it in my wardrobe. I got a little mesh skirt for when I'm not in the water and I also just dyed the suit an entirely different color. I wish I had just gotten a darker color to begin with, I may have liked it more right from the start as the booty area looks infinitely better when it doesn't match my flesh. I'm going to have to re-sew all of the hems though because the last thing I want is a wardrobe malfunction at the beach.

4.5 stars in my book! I’m 5’9”. Pre-pregnancy weight was 140lbs (size 6-8). About 156lbs now at 27 weeks pregnant. I ordered a size large to give myself room to grow and be able to wear through the third trimester. Suit fits well now and bottoms still have tons of flexibility in the belly as I hoped. The ruching allows for the excess material to hide well. The bottoms are my favorite, a little cheeky without being too revealing. Doesn’t cut into your skin at all. The cut of the top is a tad uncomfortable under the arms but still doable. I did try these bottoms on with a regular bikini top but didn’t like the look. No underwire which makes it more comfortable but less supportive if you’re larger in the chest (I’m 36-38B for reference). Straps are adjustable and removable. Has removable bra pads as well. Love that the frill covers down to where the bottoms begin. The black color is very flattering. Not sure a lighter color would flatter up top as well. Suit arrived with deodorant stains all over which was a tad gross and made me think the seller doesn’t look over their returns thoroughly. Still not a huge deal. Overall a great swimsuit! It’s a bit cheaper buying directly on Summer Mae website with their regular discounts, but Amazon offered free shipping and easy returns so I ordered here. Decided to keep! Highly recommend! :)
